title: Durability module scaffold and error taxonomy
purpose: Establish the aion::durability module subtree and the error types every other brief in the cluster returns. The taxonomy is foundational: the Recorder, Resolver, replay, and recovery all surface failures through DurabilityError, and the NonDeterminismError shape is the contract the violation-detection path fills in.
task: Create the durability module under the aion crate (crates/aion/src/durability/) with a mod.rs that only declares submodules and re-exports. Define the error taxonomy in error.rs: a DurabilityError thiserror enum and a distinct NonDeterminismError carrying enough context to locate the offending workflow code. Wire pub mod durability into the aion crate's lib.rs. This brief defines types and module structure only — no recording, replay, or resolution logic. Coordinate with AE (which owns aion's lib.rs and the rest of the crate): add only the pub mod durability declaration, do not modify unrelated engine wiring.

requirements.title: Durability module scaffold
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the durability module subtree under crates/aion/src/durability/. THE mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports and SHALL NOT contain any type, function, or trait definition.
acceptance: crates/aion/src/durability/mod.rs declares submodules seq, recorder, correlation, cursor, command, resolver, error, determinism, executor, replay, recovery (declarations may be added incrementally by later briefs, but error is declared here).
mod.rs contains no fn, struct, enum, trait, or impl item — only pub mod and pub use.
crates/aion/src/lib.rs gains exactly one new line, pub mod durability;, and nothing else in this brief.
cargo build -p aion succeeds with the new empty/partial module tree (placeholder submodules declared by later briefs are not required to exist yet beyond error).

requirements.title: DurabilityError taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define DurabilityError as a thiserror enum covering append/sequence failure (wrapping aion_store::StoreError), non-determinism violation (wrapping NonDeterminismError), and history-shape errors. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT construct any DurabilityError by panicking or via unwrap/expect.
acceptance: error.rs defines DurabilityError with at least: Store(#[from] StoreError), NonDeterminism(NonDeterminismError), and a HistoryShape variant for malformed/inconsistent recorded history (e.g. a result event with no preceding schedule).
DurabilityError derives Debug and implements Display + std::error::Error via thiserror.
No unwrap, expect, panic, todo, or unimplemented appears in error.rs.
A unit test formats one value of each variant via Display and asserts the message is non-empty and mentions the underlying cause.

requirements.title: NonDeterminismError shape
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define NonDeterminismError as a typed error carrying the WorkflowId, the sequence position of the mismatch, a description of the expected command shape, and a description of the found recorded-event shape.
acceptance: NonDeterminismError carries workflow_id: WorkflowId, seq: u64 (the cursor position of the mismatch), expected: String (or a small enum) naming the command family + correlation key the workflow issued, and found: String (or a small enum) naming the recorded event family + key.
NonDeterminismError derives Debug, implements Display via thiserror, and its Display includes the workflow id, the position, and both shapes.
NonDeterminismError derives Clone and PartialEq so tests can assert exact expected/found shapes.
A unit test constructs a NonDeterminismError and asserts its Display string contains the workflow id, the sequence position, the expected, and the found descriptors.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the Recorder, Resolver, cursor, replay, recovery, or determinism context — those are AD-002 through AD-010.
SHALL NOT modify any engine logic in the aion crate beyond adding pub mod durability to lib.rs.
SHALL NOT define the EventStore trait or any aion-core type — those are consumed from aion-store and aion-core.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes (the error unit tests).
mod.rs contains only declarations and re-exports (CO3).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
